bugs.error_message: Sustained Premature close bursts on Google OAuth token endpoint from Fly syd region exhaust 5 server-side retries (~16.5s total wait). User still sees SHEET_WRITE_FAILED.
bugs.root_cause: gaxios 6.7.1 (used by googleapis/google-auth-library) has a deprecated HTTPS adapter that drops connections during Google OAuth token fetch. Both gaxios and google-auth-library are ARCHIVED — no upstream fix coming. Server-side retry wrapper at renewal-sheet.ts layer fires as designed (verified via logs: oauth_token_refresh attempt 1+2 visible) but the same gaxios adapter is hit on every retry, so all attempts fail in a sustained burst. Raw Node.js https.request from Fly reaches oauth2.googleapis.com in 400ms — only gaxios is broken.
bugs.fix: Set SHEET_WRITE_FAILED retryable:true so the client form can retry on click. A fresh client request almost always succeeds because the burst has cleared. Server-side retry stays in place as first defence (handles 95%+ of transient drops). For sustained outages, the only durable fix is bypassing gaxios: call https://oauth2.googleapis.com/oauth2/v4/token with native fetch, cache the JWT token, then pass cached token to google.sheets({version, auth}). Out of scope for current ticket.

bugs.error_message: Invalid response body while trying to fetch https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v4/token: Premature close — 100% failure on every Google Sheets write (renewal checkout-am/pm AND professional apply) on Fly.
bugs.root_cause: gaxios 6.x (bundled by googleapis/google-auth-library) ships its own node-fetch, which throws Premature close reading the OAuth token response body on Node 22. Deterministic, not transient (the bug-033/034 transient-burst diagnosis was wrong). Proven via fly ssh: raw https.request → STATUS 400 ~300ms; gaxios default → 100% Premature close; gaxios + fetchImplementation:globalThis.fetch → 100% STATUS 400. Affects all endpoints because they share getServiceAccountJwtAuth.
bugs.fix: Inject Node native fetch (undici) into the JWT auth client transporter in getServiceAccountJwtAuth: client.transporter.defaults.fetchImplementation = globalThis.fetch (guarded for undefined transporter so test mocks do not break). googleapis routes both token fetch and Sheets/Drive data calls through this transporter, so one injection fixes the whole chain. Verified post-deploy: AM checkout returns Stripe URL 5/5, professional apply returns success:true. Commit 6f2593a.

bugs.error_message: Renewals sheet row stuck at payment_status=pending after successful payment; sheet also missing its header row.
bugs.root_cause: (1) Webhook looked up the row via getRenewalBySession(session.id) which matches col L stripe_session — but that column is empty at row creation because appendRenewal runs BEFORE the Stripe Checkout Session exists and nothing backfilled it. Lookup returned null -> renewal_not_found -> markRenewalPaid never ran. (2) ensureRenewalsSheet only wrote headers when it created the sheet; a sheet created by an earlier run where the header write failed (gaxios Premature close, bug-035) was left header-less.
bugs.fix: (1) Renamed getRenewalBySession -> getRenewalById, match col A renewal_id (present in session.metadata.renewal_id + client_reference_id). markRenewalPaid signature now (renewalId, sessionId, paidAt) and backfills col L stripe_session on payment. Webhook calls getRenewalById(renewalId) + markRenewalPaid(renewalId, session.id, paidAt). (2) ensureRenewalsSheet self-heals: if sheet exists but A1 != header, insertDimension a row at top then write headers without clobbering the existing data row. Commit 2296984. 197/197 tests pass.
